title: The One with Rachel's Big Kiss

jokes.text: Hey, out of all of us, who do you think will get married next?
jokes.setup: Opening question about who will marry next among the group
jokes.punchline: Immediate obvious answer that follows
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for obvious punchline - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Probably Monica and Chandler.
jokes.setup: Previous question about marriage
jokes.punchline: States the obvious couple choice
jokes.explanation: Straightforward response - economical but not creative

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Melissa Worbert. I don't think I have the energy for this.
jokes.setup: Spotting someone from the past
jokes.punchline: Immediate dread about social interaction energy
jokes.explanation: Relatable social anxiety moment that feels authentic to character

jokes.text: Ray-Ray Green?
jokes.setup: College reunion context
jokes.punchline: Overly familiar sorority-style nickname
jokes.explanation: Establishes sorority girl character type through nickname

jokes.text: You've been MIA for seven sorority newsletters.
jokes.setup: College friend reunion
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific tracking of newsletter participation
jokes.explanation: Specific detail that reveals character's overeager sorority devotion

jokes.text: Oh, shut up more! Are you friends with Ralph?
jokes.setup: Rachel working at Ralph Lauren
jokes.punchline: Escalating excited disbelief with 'shut up more'
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the 'shut up' exclamation - quotable

jokes.text: You're having dinner with me.
jokes.setup: Asking about dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Pushy declaration rather than invitation
jokes.explanation: Shows pushy sorority personality through demanding rather than asking

jokes.text: Oh, no, heh, that's an old card. Um, I got out of that to do something where I could... help people and make a difference. ... I'm a party planner.
jokes.setup: Claiming to want to help people and make a difference
jokes.punchline: Reveals job as party planner - hardly world-changing
jokes.explanation: Well-structured ironic reveal - the pause builds expectation for something meaningful

jokes.text: Hey guys, look who's back. It's Ray-Ray.
jokes.setup: Rachel previously dreaded this interaction
jokes.punchline: Now enthusiastically using the sorority nickname
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly Rachel got swept up in the sorority energy

jokes.text: Who you what? Who you what?
jokes.setup: Someone cuts off mid-sentence about Rachel and Melissa
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly desperate need to know
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction - his horniness driving the repetition

jokes.text: They were lovers.
jokes.setup: Build-up about Rachel and Melissa's past
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly dramatic declaration
jokes.explanation: Monica's deadpan delivery of overly dramatic term for a college kiss

jokes.text: So that's two of my wives.
jokes.setup: Learning Rachel kissed Melissa
jokes.punchline: Ross realizing pattern of his wives kissing women
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that creates unexpected pattern - very Ross-like observation

jokes.text: Why don't we just put me with a Manhattan in my hand... talking to the cute bartender.
jokes.setup: Monica placing Rachel on wedding seating chart
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately imagining herself flirting scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rachel character moment - always thinking about romance

jokes.text: Chandler, relax. It's not a contest. Certainly not a close one.
jokes.setup: Monica comforting Chandler about having fewer wedding guests
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts comfort with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica - tries to be supportive but can't resist the competitive dig

jokes.text: Stop picturing it!
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about Rachel kissing Melissa
jokes.punchline: Rachel reading Ross's obvious mental imagery
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - we all know what Ross is thinking

jokes.text: Well, if this is the wedding hall, then, um... your parents would be over here at home in Queens.
jokes.setup: Joey asking where his parents will sit at wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing to empty space - they're not invited
jokes.explanation: Monica's matter-of-fact delivery of the harsh reality

jokes.text: Look, I thought parents were coming. Your parents are coming. Chandler's parents are coming. Ross' parents are coming. Ross' parents are my parents.
jokes.setup: Joey defending his assumption about parents attending
jokes.punchline: Monica realizing they share parents
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals the obvious flaw in Joey's logic

jokes.text: You just want more blue pins.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting they invite his parents
jokes.punchline: Monica seeing through his motivation
jokes.explanation: Monica correctly identifying Joey's competitive motive

jokes.text: Maybe you'll order a little sangria.
jokes.setup: Rachel might have to sit at the bar
jokes.punchline: Ross referencing the drink from the kiss story
jokes.explanation: Ross can't let go of the college story - very in character

jokes.text: I'm Monica's maid of honor. Okay, don't try to blue-pin me.
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting Rachel as his wedding guest
jokes.punchline: Rachel accusing him of trying to steal her for his guest count
jokes.explanation: Clever verb usage of the pin system - 'blue-pin' as competitive action

jokes.text: Might I suggest watching a little more ESPN... and a little less E!
jokes.setup: Ross getting excited about red carpet glamour
jokes.punchline: Chandler questioning his masculinity via TV preferences
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler snark - specific and character-appropriate

jokes.text: He plays for the Yankees. Seriously, ESPN.
jokes.setup: Ross not knowing Paul O'Neil
jokes.punchline: Rachel reinforcing Chandler's ESPN suggestion
jokes.explanation: Piling on Ross's sports ignorance - functional callback

jokes.text: Ah, it's not just that. I would be England's most powerful weapon. A jet-setting heartbreaker on Her Majesty's secret service. A man who fears no one, with a license to kill.
jokes.setup: Ross wanting to wear Pierce Brosnan's tux
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about becoming James Bond
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross nerdiness - completely earnest about ridiculous fantasy

jokes.text: Would Monica let me wear this?
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate James Bond fantasy
jokes.punchline: Reality check - he needs girlfriend's permission
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of his Bond fantasy with domestic reality

jokes.text: No, I'm not wearing a tux. What are you gonna wear? Multi-colored robes. Ooh, and maybe a hat.
jokes.setup: Question about wedding attire
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's bizarre outfit choice for formal wedding
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe - completely oblivious to wedding formality

jokes.text: I won't talk. You'll just hear my video camera.
jokes.setup: Joey wanting to come to Rachel's dinner
jokes.punchline: Planning to secretly record lesbian interaction
jokes.explanation: Very Joey - his horniness overriding all social norms

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm sure that happened. It did. Sure.
jokes.setup: Rachel insisting she kissed Melissa
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's complete disbelief
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's sarcastic disbelief - her tone sells the joke

jokes.text: Which means she had a couple spritzers and a peck on the cheek.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing passionate college kiss
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's realistic translation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Rachel's dramatic story with mundane reality

jokes.text: Why are you taking this away from me?
jokes.setup: Phoebe doubting her story
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate plea to keep her one wild story
jokes.explanation: Rachel's vulnerability about having no exciting stories

jokes.text: Okay. I just didn't know that you were a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Rachel insisting the kiss happened
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logical but wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's matter-of-fact misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: Vanilla? I'm not vanilla. I do lots of crazy things. I mean, I got drunk and married in Vegas. To Ross.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending against being called vanilla
jokes.punchline: Her wild example is marrying her ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Great deflation - her rebellion was with the safest possible person

jokes.text: You should get inside my head.
jokes.setup: Phoebe can't picture Rachel kissing a woman
jokes.punchline: Joey offering his vivid imagination
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - always ready to share his sexual fantasies

jokes.text: A freakishly thin date with a hanger for a head?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking Monica to guess what he got
jokes.punchline: Monica's brutal assessment of his dating history
jokes.explanation: Specific and harsh Monica observation about Chandler's type

jokes.text: Made expressly for Val Kilmer, and worn by him in the hit film... that Batman film he was in.
jokes.setup: Chandler building up the Batman connection
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the movie name
jokes.explanation: Undermines his excitement with inability to recall basic details

jokes.text: Hello! You have the most special thing of all. You are marrying the woman you love. [SPEAKING GIBBERISH]
jokes.setup: Chandler's romantic speech about marriage
jokes.punchline: Ross's incomprehensible response
jokes.explanation: Ross so focused on the tux he can't process meaningful sentiment

jokes.text: Pretty please? Not very, uh, 007.
jokes.setup: Ross begging for the Batman tux
jokes.punchline: Chandler pointing out unmanly pleading
jokes.explanation: Good point about Ross's uncharacteristic begging

jokes.text: You slept with my sister. That was pretty 007.
jokes.setup: Chandler criticizing Ross's behavior
jokes.punchline: Ross defending Chandler's past actions as Bond-like
jokes.explanation: Clever reframing of their relationship history in spy terms

jokes.text: That's too bad. It's true. But too bad.
jokes.setup: Joey's parents feeling unwanted
jokes.punchline: Monica's brutal honesty about their feelings being correct
jokes.explanation: Peak Monica - acknowledging harsh truth while pretending sympathy

jokes.text: Is it her fault if some didn't make it to you?
jokes.setup: Monica saying Mrs. Tribbiani hasn't sent lasagnas
jokes.punchline: Joey implying he ate them before they reached Monica
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - can't resist food even meant for others

jokes.text: They hate the post office. And the Irish. But I don't think you could blame it on them.
jokes.setup: Discussing blaming postal service
jokes.punchline: Random addition of ethnic prejudice then logical retreat
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's family's prejudices and his attempt at logic

jokes.text: What are they, Irish?
jokes.setup: Monica complaining about postal service
jokes.punchline: Accidentally echoing the Tribbiani prejudice
jokes.explanation: Monica unknowingly playing into the family prejudice

jokes.text: Yes. I was a, um, Thigh Mega Tampon.
jokes.setup: Asked about sorority membership
jokes.punchline: Made-up inappropriate sorority name
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe absurdity - completely inappropriate fake Greek letters

jokes.text: Yeah, we were huge too. But they had to shut us down... when Regina Phalangie died of alcohol poisoning.
jokes.setup: Continuing fake sorority story
jokes.punchline: Dark fake backstory with her recurring fake name
jokes.explanation: Escalates absurd story with dark turn and callback to her fake identity

jokes.text: Isn't it a shame when one girl ruins it for the whole bunch?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's fake story about alcohol poisoning death
jokes.punchline: Melissa's completely inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Melissa's clueless sorority girl response to tragic story

jokes.text: Remember we... Come on, we had sarongs on and the coconut bikini tops. We went back to the house and got really silly... and we made out.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to get Melissa to remember
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific details that sound made up
jokes.explanation: Rachel desperately adding details that make story sound faker

jokes.text: Wow, Ray-Ray, I have no idea what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Rachel's elaborate recreation of memory
jokes.punchline: Complete denial from Melissa
jokes.explanation: Melissa's polite but firm denial undermines Rachel's story

jokes.text: Well, he did manage to keep his identity secret for a long time.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying Ross is Batman
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry observation about Ross's secret-keeping
jokes.explanation: Clever observation connecting Batman's secrecy to Ross's character

jokes.text: Batman has Robin.
jokes.setup: Comparing Bond getting ladies to Batman
jokes.punchline: Implying Batman has gay relationship
jokes.explanation: Obvious innuendo - not particularly clever but lands the implication

jokes.text: Actually I don't like martinis. A Yoo-Hoo with a funny straw? Ooh, yum.
jokes.setup: Monica offering Bond's signature drink
jokes.punchline: Ross preferring childish chocolate drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Bond fantasy with Ross's childish preferences

jokes.text: Right, the wedding. Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Joey calling wedding 'the show'
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction revealing his performer mindset
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey treating sacred ceremony like entertainment gig

jokes.text: My part's in the beginning. I'm not in the rest of the show... Wedding.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining scheduling conflict
jokes.punchline: Keeps slipping back into show business terms
jokes.explanation: Joey can't stop thinking in entertainment terms

jokes.text: In Joey Tribbiani, you get a minister and an entertainer. I'm a 'ministainer.'
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his value as wedding officiant
jokes.punchline: Made-up portmanteau word
jokes.explanation: Silly but memorable word creation - very Joey

jokes.text: I don't remember a lot of things that never happened.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking how she can't remember kissing
jokes.punchline: Logical but cutting response
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic wrapped in devastating deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Our coconuts kept knocking together. Somewhere Joey's head is exploding.
jokes.setup: Rachel describing physical details of alleged encounter
jokes.punchline: Phoebe noting Joey's reaction to sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Good awareness of Joey's predictable reaction to sexual details

jokes.text: Maybe I passed out and you did stuff to me while I was sleeping.
jokes.setup: Melissa denying the kiss happened
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggesting sexual assault scenario
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable territory - desperation making Rachel inappropriate

jokes.text: Yes. I find it to be something of a conversation piece. Between you and? Gunther.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Ross carrying invitation
jokes.punchline: Ross admitting limited social circle
jokes.explanation: Ross's pathetic admission about his social interactions

jokes.text: I could see 00 and 7 in those things.
jokes.setup: Ross's pants being too tight
jokes.punchline: Chandler seeing Bond's number through fabric
jokes.explanation: Clever visual joke about pants being so tight everything shows

jokes.text: Holy double-vented comfort, Batman!
jokes.setup: Ross putting on Batman jacket
jokes.punchline: Batman catchphrase adapted for clothing comfort
jokes.explanation: Decent adaptation of Robin's catchphrase for mundane context

jokes.text: The only super power you have is the slightly heightened sense of smell.
jokes.setup: Discovery that tux is from blind guy movie
jokes.punchline: Chandler defining pathetic superpower
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of superhero fantasy with realistic blind person ability

jokes.text: You want your mom at the altar screaming racial slurs? I don't care.
jokes.setup: Monica giving up on wedding planning control
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of what she'd allow
jokes.explanation: Monica's breaking point expressed through absurd worst-case scenario

jokes.text: I wear it like this when I marry you guys. And then this... is for party time.
jokes.setup: Joey showing wedding officiant outfit
jokes.punchline: Reversible formal/party wear reveal
jokes.explanation: Joey's practical approach to formal/party wear transition

jokes.text: That night was the one wild thing I've ever done in my life. I won't let you take it away from me.
jokes.setup: Melissa leaving after denying the kiss
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate clinging to her only wild story
jokes.explanation: Rachel's vulnerability about her boring life driving desperate behavior

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You love me.
jokes.setup: Rachel kisses Melissa to prove the college story
jokes.punchline: Melissa misinterpreting desperation as romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Great twist - Rachel's evidence backfires completely

jokes.text: I can still hear the coconuts knocking together, I... I didn't want to tell you, because I didn't think you'd return my love.
jokes.setup: Melissa now claiming to remember everything
jokes.punchline: Elaborate romantic fantasy based on Rachel's desperate kiss
jokes.explanation: Melissa suddenly remembering everything when it serves her romantic fantasy

jokes.text: I'm just a good kisser. Shut up.
jokes.setup: Melissa saying the kiss proved love
jokes.punchline: Rachel's proud boast followed by Melissa's signature phrase
jokes.explanation: Rachel's ego momentarily surfacing before reality hits

jokes.text: I don't picture your face when I make love to my boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Melissa confessing her feelings to Rachel
jokes.punchline: TMI detail about her fantasy life
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate oversharing that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: What the hell was that? Just wanted to see what all the fuss was about. And? I've had better.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suddenly kisses Rachel after watching drama
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissive review of Rachel's kissing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - casual about everything, brutally honest review

jokes.text: Well, you know what they say? 23rd time's a charm.
jokes.setup: Chandler finally finding a tux that fits
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated number of attempts needed
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation of familiar saying - functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Diane Keaton.
jokes.setup: Chandler looking handsome in mystery tux
jokes.punchline: Reveals it belonged to female celebrity
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and punchline - builds expectation then completely subverts it

tmdb_overview: Rachel reconnects with college friend Melissa and shares more than friendship. Monica arranges wedding seating. Chandler and Ross dispute wearing a celebrity tux.
